stocks.note: *Numbers as of market close on March 4th at 4:00 PM ET
stocks.news: Markets edged higher on Tuesday as investors shook off earlier geopolitical jitters. The S&P 500 rose to 6,869.50, the Dow climbed to 48,501.27, and the NASDAQ held steady at 22,516.69. Traders are now eyeing upcoming jobs data and Fed commentary for direction this week.

economy.commentary:
The 10-year Treasury yield ticked up to 4.14%, signaling continued caution around the Fed's rate path. Gold pulled back sharply to $5,134.76 and silver dropped to $82.82, retreating from recent highs as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East showed signs of easing.

Despite the pullback, precious metals remain elevated on a year-over-year basis, reflecting persistent inflation hedging demand and central bank buying.

crypto.commentary:
Bitcoin retreated to $68,573 as profit-taking continued following last week's rally, while Ethereum held flat at $2,012. XRP was the standout, climbing to $1.42 amid renewed optimism around the CLARITY Act — a bipartisan bill that could provide a clear regulatory framework for digital assets.

Analysts note that if the CLARITY Act gains traction in Congress, it could trigger a major repricing of XRP and other altcoins that have suffered from regulatory uncertainty.

real_estate.homebuilder: Eastwood Homes is expanding its Greenville Division with several new communities across the Upstate region of South Carolina, with sales slated to begin in March — a sign that national builders remain confident in housing demand despite elevated rates.
real_estate.commentary:
The Bay Area housing market is currently defined by a "steady but not overheating" phase, with housing inventory projected to rise roughly 10%. While detached single-family homes remain highly competitive, the condo and townhome segment has shown signs of flattening or slight decline.

📍 Alameda County:
• Median Sale Price: $925,000 — a 2.4% increase year-over-year
• Homes Sold: Dropped by nearly 5%, reflecting cautious buyer activity
• Market Outlook: Entering the critical March–May planning window with steady but measured demand

📍 Santa Clara County:
• Median Sale Price: $1,299,000
• Inventory: Sharp month-over-month decline of approximately 25%
• Key Trend: Single-family homes priced between $2M–$3M remain highly competitive, while condos and townhouses are expected to stay flat due to increased supply from long-term owners looking to sell before capital gains exclusion windows close

Latest General News in the Bay Area:

📰 Community Support in Milpitas: Local organizations recently rallied to support unhoused and immigrant residents following shifts in regional policy.

📰 Legislative Push: Assemblymember Alex Lee has introduced Assembly Bill 1675, aimed at deterring immigration enforcement and removing tax breaks for private detention centers — part of a broader push by Silicon Valley lawmakers.

📰 Farewell to Koong's: The Milpitas community is mourning the closure of Koong's, a staple Chinese restaurant that served the area for over four decades.

📰 Heroic Rescue: Milpitas first responders were recently recognized for rescuing a family from an apartment fire.
